channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Afghan and Pakistani delegations head to Doha for crisis talks
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — Afghan and Pakistani delegations are heading to the Qatari capital, Doha, hoping to defuse the deadliest crisis between them in several years, after more than a week of fighting killed dozens of people and injured hundreds on both sides.
item.dc:creator: RIAZAT BUTT, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: From barracks to palace: Soldiers who led military coups to become state leaders
item.description: JOHANNESBURG (AP) — Weeks of nationwide Gen Z protests in Madagascar sparked by power and water shortages escalated and led to a military coup that forced President Andry Rajoelina into exile. Army officer Col. Michael Randrianirina has been sworn in as the new leader of the Indian Ocean nation.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE GUMEDE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Israel identifies the remains of one more hostage
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israel says the remains of another hostage that Hamas handed over the day before have been identified as Eliyahu Margalit, as the Palestinian militant group looks for more bodies under the rubble in the Gaza Strip and urges more aid to be allowed into the embattled enclave.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Alaska storm damage so bad many evacuees won’t go home for at least 18 months, governor says
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — Damage to remote Alaska villages hammered by flooding last weekend is so extreme that many of the more than 2,000 people displaced won’t be able to return to their homes for at least 18 months, Gov.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN and GENE JOHNSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: RECALL: Walmart warns dressers could fall over
item.description: Walmart has issued a recall for a product that the United States Consumer Product Safety Commission says poses a "risk of serious injury or death.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, KREX 5

item.title: Mother of missing girl previously not seen for 1 year being 'uncooperative,' sheriff says
item.description: The Santa Barbara County Sheriff's Office is continuing its search for 9-year-old Melodee Buzzard, who was not previously seen for more than a year and hadn't been photographed in at least two.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KRON4

item.title: Dozens of costumed canines get their chance to trick-or-treating at Howloween event in Michigan
item.description: Dogs dressed up as everything from Elvis Presley to Scooby Doo’s Mystery Machine went trick-or-treating in Michigan.
item.dc:creator: Mike Householder, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The other Alaska airlift: Volunteers save dogs from a flooded Alaska village, 1 tiny plane at a time
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — The special delivery arrived in a plastic storage box after a chartered flight in bouncy single-propeller plane. Veterinarian Susan Shaffer Sookram snipped the zip ties securing the lid and greeted the cargo: four dogs, one with a gray collar bearing its name, Happy.
item.dc:creator: CEDAR ATTANASIO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Trump says he has commuted sentence of former US Rep. George Santos in federal fraud case
item.description: President Donald Trump said Friday he had commuted the sentence of former U.S. Rep. George Santos, who is serving more than seven years in federal prison after pleading guilty to fraud and identity theft charges.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump says he has commuted sentence of former US Rep. George Santos in federal fraud case
item.description: President Donald Trump said Friday he had commuted the sentence of former U.S. Rep. George Santos, who is serving more than seven years in federal prison after pleading guilty to fraud and identity theft charges.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: News stories show that reporters may have left the Pentagon, but they haven't stopped working
item.description: Two days after dozens of journalists left their desks at the Pentagon behind rather than agree to government-imposed rules on how they report about the U.S. military, it's apparent they haven't stopped working. Reporters have relied on sources to break and add nuance to stories about U.S.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Philadelphia officials seek tips about Kada Scott, who disappeared 2 weeks ago
item.description: Authorities in Philadelphia say they are no closer to locating a missing 23-year-old woman who disappeared from her nursing home job nearly two weeks ago.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: LA county reaches tentative $828M agreement to settle 400 more claims of sexual abuse
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Los Angeles County has reached a tentative $828 million agreement to settle more than 400 additional sexual abuse claims against county employees, months after approving the largest sexual abuse settlement in U.S. history, officials said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Indiana University fires student newspaper adviser who refused to block news stories
item.description: Tension between Indiana University and its student newspaper flared this week with the elimination of the outlet's print editions and the firing of a faculty adviser, who refused an order to keep news stories out of a homecoming edition.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump finalizes $1.6B loan for power lines initiated by Biden
item.description: The Trump administration is finalizing a $1.6 billion loan for power lines in Indiana, Michigan, Ohio, Oklahoma, and West Virginia, finding itself in rare agreement with its predecessor on funding an energy project.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, NewsNation

item.title: Family members share Diane Keaton's cause of death
item.description: Family members of legendary actress Diane Keaton, who passed away Saturday at the age of 79, revealed the Oscar-winning actress died during a battle with pneumonia, according to multiple media reports.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, Fox 5

item.title: The world is on track to add 57 superhot days a year, but it could have been worse: Study
item.description: The world is on track to add nearly two months of dangerous superhot days each year by the end of the century.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: NHC watching Caribbean for potential tropical development
item.description: The National Hurricane Center is watching the Caribbean Sea for potential tropical development next week.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Some protein powders, shakes contain high lead levels, report suggests
item.description: Out of 23 brands tested, researchers found that more than two-thirds contained more lead in a single serving than is considered safe to ingest in a day.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, ABC 4

item.title: Capitol Police called to investigate US flag with swastika in GOP congressional office
item.description: Rep. Dave Taylor (R-Ohio) on Wednesday condemned a symbol on the wall of his Washington, D.C., office, which was reportedly a swastika in the middle of a U.S. flag.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mancini, KRON4

item.title: Journalists turn in access badges, exit Pentagon rather than agree to new reporting rules
item.description: Dozens of reporters turned in access badges and exited the Pentagon on Wednesday rather than agree to government-imposed restrictions on their work, pushing journalists who cover the American military further from the seat of its power.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Flight delays may get worse soon, experts warn
item.description: Flight delays, long security lines and ground stops could all get worse this week as some essential airport workers receive their final paycheck under the ongoing government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WGN 9

item.title: Faulty engineering led to implosion of Titan submersible headed to Titanic wreckage, NTSB finds
item.description: PORTLAND, Maine (AP) — Faulty engineering led to the implosion of an experimental submersible that killed five people on the way to the wreck of the Titanic, the National Transportation Safety Board concluded in a report Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE and HOLLY RAMER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A discarded straw leads to murder charges in 1984 killing of New York teen
item.description: MINEOLA, N.Y. (AP) — Four decades after prosecutors sent the wrong men to prison for the rape and murder of a 16-year-old Long Island girl, DNA obtained from a discarded straw has led to the indictment of a new suspect.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New Oklahoma schools superintendent rescinds mandate for Bible instruction in schools
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — Oklahoma’s new public schools superintendent announced Wednesday he is rescinding a mandate from his predecessor that forced schools to place Bibles in classrooms and incorporate the book into lesson plans for students.
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Young Republicans call for immediate resignations after report on GOP group chat
item.description: The chat also featured racial slurs and reportedly said that rape was “epic,” according to the report.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Hispanic authors and bookstores push for representation in publishing
item.description: Authors, readers and publishing industry experts lament the underrepresentation of Hispanic stories in the mainstream world of books, but have found new ways to elevate the literature and resolve misunderstandings.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Social Security cost-of-living announcement delayed by government shutdown
item.description: The postponement of the announcement is the most recent example of how the government shutdown has made it more difficult for people to plan out their finances.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: DHS: US Coast Guard to get paid despite government shutdown
item.description: Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem on Tuesday announced that U.S. Coast Guard employees will get paid this week, despite the government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, WGN 9

item.title: Instagram says it's safeguarding teens by limiting them to PG-13 content
item.description: Teenagers on Instagram will be restricted to seeing PG-13 content by default and won't be able to change their settings without a parent's permission, Meta announced on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: WATCH: Arsonist attacks Pennsylvania governor's residence; suspect pleads guilty
item.description: On April 13, Cody Balmer climbed the fence of the Governor's Residence in Harrisburg, carrying a bag that contained Molotov cocktails and a sledgehammer.
item.dc:creator: Brady Doran, Fox 5

item.title: Supreme Court takes up Republican attack on Voting Rights Act in case over Black representation
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A Republican attack on a core provision of the Voting Rights Act that is designed to protect racial minorities comes to the Supreme Court this week, more than a decade after the justices knocked out another pillar of the 60-year-old law.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4
